headline: Opposition MPs allocated zero committee chairmanships despite convention
context: The government allocated all 14 parliamentary select committee chairmanships to ruling coalition MPs in 2025, breaking with the convention of giving PAC chair to the opposition.

cards.big: 14 committee chairs — all government MPs, zero opposition, breaking 20-year convention
cards.sub: Even the Public Accounts Committee chair went to a ruling coalition member

cards.big: Standing Order 77 gives Speaker sole discretion over committee composition
cards.sub: No legal requirement for opposition representation in committee leadership

cards.big: UK convention gives 40% of committee chairs to opposition parties
cards.sub: Westminster systems function better when oversight roles are shared across parties

cards.big: PAC under opposition chair in 2018-2020 produced 23 critical reports
cards.sub: Opposition-led oversight was demonstrably more rigorous in holding government to account

cards.big: When the government chairs its own oversight committees, accountability becomes self-review
Should PAC and key committee chairs automatically go to opposition MPs by Standing Order?
